title: Tulja Bhavani Temple
tagline: Tulja Bhavani Temple — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: Shree Tulaja Bhavani Temple (Marathi: श्री तुळजाभवानी मंदिर), is a 12th century CE Hindu temple dedicated to goddess Bhavani.
overview.description: Shree Tulaja Bhavani Temple (Marathi: श्री तुळजाभवानी मंदिर), is a 12th century CE Hindu temple dedicated to goddess Bhavani. It was built in 12th century CE by Mahamandaleshwara Māradadeva of the Kadamb dynasty. Considered as one of the 51 Shakti Pithas, it is located on the banks of Mandakini River and Bori Dam in Yamunachala Hill of Balaghat Range of Tuljapur, which is 45 km northeast of Solapur, in Dharashiv district of Maharashtra in India. This Tuljapur Bhavani temple, along with Renuka temple at Mahur (330 km northeast of Tuljapur), Mahalaxmi temple at Kolhapur (275 southwest of Tuljapu

topPlaces.name: Tuljapur
topPlaces.description: Tuljapur is a town with a municipal council in Dharashiv District in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is the administrative seat of Tuljapur taluka.

items.note: Tuljapur is a town with a municipal council in Dharashiv District in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is the administrative seat of Tuljapur taluka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tulja Bhavani Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tulja Bhavani Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tulja Bhavani Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Nanded Airport (181 km) and the nearest railway station is Solapur Junction (44 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tulja Bhavani Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tulja Bhavani Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tulja Bhavani Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Tulja Bhavani Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
